ground_truth.title: MBA 2025 - Business school rankings from the Financial Times
ground_truth.main_content: MBA 2025 Rankings - Financial Times Business School Rankings. Published: February 16 2025. Total Schools: 100. The ranking table displays business schools ranked by MBA program, including Eada Business School Barcelona (rank 100), Miami Herbert Business School (rank 99), Sungkyunkwan University GSB (rank 98), University of Glasgow: Adam Smith (rank 97), Frankfurt School of Finance and Management (rank 96), University of British Columbia: Sauder (rank 95), McGill University: Desautels (rank 94), Copenhagen Business School (rank 93), Hult International Business School (rank 92), University of Edinburgh Business School (rank 91), Audencia (rank 90), GEM (rank 89), Indian Institute of Management Kozhikode (rank 86), Western University: Ivey (rank 86), Durham University Business School (rank 86), XLRI — Xavier School of Management (rank 83), Babson College: Olin (rank 83), Brigham Young University: Marriott (rank 83), Cranfield School of Management (rank 82), ESMT Berlin (rank 80), William & Mary: Mason (rank 80), Queen's University: Smith (rank 79), Fordham University: Gabelli (rank 78), The Lisbon MBA Catolica | Nova (rank 77), University of Toronto: Rotman (rank 76), University of Notre Dame: Mendoza (rank 75), Boston University Questrom School of Business (rank 74), University College Dublin: Smurfit (rank 73), Indian Institute of Management Lucknow (rank 71), Mannheim Business School (rank 71), Texas A&M University: Mays (rank 70), Indian Institute of Management Indore (rank 69), AGSM at UNSW Business School (rank 67), Bayes Business School (rank 67), WHU – Otto Beisheim School of Management (rank 66), Chinese University of Hong Kong (rank 65), EDHEC Business School (rank 64), and many more schools continuing through the full list of 100 ranked institutions.
with: MBA 2025 Rankings - Financial Times Business School Rankings.
The ranking table displays business schools ranked by MBA program.
Schools include Eada Business School Barcelona, Miami Herbert Business School, and University of Glasgow.
This is the annual Financial Times MBA ranking of the world's top 100 business schools.
